text: It is not things that disturb us, but our judgments about them.
author: Epictetus

text:
What is strength?
To be able to create miracles?
To be able to overcome misery & pain?
To be able to regain lost glory?
Or all of these?
author: Unknown

text: You must become who you are.
author: Nietzsche

text: "You need an alter ego. A version of you that's limitless. Make your weaknesses their strength. Make your insecurity, their confidence. Give them a name. Give them traits. Create an amazing Character. When you need them, let them take over. And eventually, you won't need to pretend. It
author: Unknown

text: According to psychology, the degree to which a person can grow is directly proportional to the amount of truth they can accept-about themselves, without running away.
author: Unknown

text: Emperor Fushima‘says
author: Unknown

text: Yea, sure coffee never betrays, and solitude never demands— but now I catch myself wishing you were there, as if solitude itself had learned your name, and coffee has started to taste more like you
author: Rushi

text: Physicist Love
author: Unknown

text:
Maybe You weren't as beautiful as I believed, nor as precious as l imagined.
Maybe it was never You who was flawless, but my eyes that dressed You in perfection.
And now, as the illusion fades, I wonder if I ever loved You-or just the picture I painted of You in my mind.
author: Unknown

text: If it is not right, do not do it.
author: Marcus Aurelius

text: Luck is what happens when preparation meets opportunity.
author: Seneca

text: Hope is a cruel joke played on mankind by fate.
author: Unknown

text: You did what you did. I feit how I felt, we are where we are, it is what it is.
author: Unknown

text: If an injury has to be done to a man it should be so severe that his vengeance need not be feared.
author: Niccolò Machiavelli

text: Guilt is a pointless emotion, no matter how none sees it.
author: Unknown

text: In order to grow, a part of you must die?
author: Unknown

text: Men judge more by the eye than by the hand.
author: Niccolò Machiavelli

text: Never was anything great achieved without danger.
author: Niccolò Machiavelli

text: This too shall pass.
author: Unknown

text: If a man knows not to which port he sails, no wind is favorable.
author: Seneca

text: Time will tell.
author: Unknown

text: The ends justify the means.
author: Niccolò Machiavelli

text: Wealth consists not in having great possessions, but in having few wants.
author: Epictetus

text: Thinking of you is a poison I drink often.
author: Unknown

text: You have power over your mind — not outside events.
author: Marcus Aurelius

text: Picture the end.
author: Unknown

text: The wise man does at once what the fool does finally.
author: Niccolò Machiavelli

text: It is better to be feared than loved, if you cannot be both.
author: Niccolò Machiavelli

text: The only true wisdom is knowing you know nothing.
author: Socrates

text: Therefore any cruelty has to be executed at once, so that the less it is tasted, the less it offends; while benefits must be dispensed little by little, so that they will be savored all the more.
author: Niccolò Machiavelli

text: He who has a why to live can bear almost any how.
author: Nietzsche

text: Whosoever desires constant success must change his conduct with the times.
author: Niccolò Machiavelli

text: Everyone sees what you appear to be; few know what you truly are.
author: Niccolò Machiavelli

text: No man is free who is not master of himself.
author: Epictetus

text: The promise given was a necessity of the past; the word broken is a necessity of the present.
author: Niccolò Machiavelli

text: Difficulties strengthen the mind.
author: Seneca

text: Accept the fact that at the end of the day, there will be no one with you
author: Unknown

text: First say to yourself what you would be; then do what you have to do.
author: Epictetus

text: Waste no more time arguing what a good person should be. Be one
author: Marcus Aurelius

text: How long are you going to wait before you demand the best for yourself?
author: Epictetus

text: Make the best use of what is in your power.
author: Epictetus

text: Whatever happens to you has been waiting to happen since the beginning of time.  The twining strands of fate wove both of them together: your own existence and the things that happen to you.
author: Marcus Aurelius

text: The soul becomes dyed with the color of its thoughts.
author: Marcus Aurelius

text: Lost SomeWhere…. Somewhere Between the emotional and emotionless.
author: Unknown

text: Whoever fights monsters should see to it that in the process he does not become a monster. And if you gaze long enough into an abyss, the abyss will gaze back into you.
author: Friedrich Nietzsche

text: Be tolerant with others and strict with yourself.
author: Marcus Aurelius

text: Happiness depends upon ourselves.
author: Aristotle

text: To be trusted is a greater compliment than being loved.
author: Unknown

text: i hope i die warmed by the life that i tried to live
